MN-rochester.description: Rochester provides a healthy blend of amenities and affordability, enchanting everyone who call this Midwestern marvel home. Natural beauty, world-class healthcare and a strong sense of community contribute to the high quality of life Rochester residents have grown accustomed to.

sections.location: Rochester is a welcoming city tucked away in the state’s southeastern region, about 85 miles southeast of Minneapolis. It’s not directly on a major interstate but is easily accessible via U.S. Route 52. The nearest airport, Rochester International Airport, is just south of the city, making travel a breeze. When you’re ready to venture out, here’s how close you are to some major nearby cities: Minneapolis is just 85 miles northwest, where you can shop ’til you drop at the Mall of America or explore the Walker Art Center. St. Paul sits 80 miles northwest, tempting with the Minnesota State Capitol and the Science Museum of Minnesota. Des Moines, IA, is about 220 miles southwest, home to the Iowa State Fair and the artsy Pappajohn Sculpture Park.
sections.economy: Ask around, and you’ll often hear that the local job market is a testament to the area’s economic vitality. Home to the renowned Mayo Clinic, one of the world’s leading medical institutions, new residents often find plenty of opportunities in industries such as health care, research and other medical-related fields. Beyond health care, major sectors include manufacturing, education and technology. The cost of living in Rochester is slightly lower than the national average. The median home value in Rochester is $291,249, and the average household income is $129,392. Minnesota residents pay a state income tax, and local residents can expect an 8.13% sales tax rate in 2025. Who are the major health care providers in Rochester, MN? Unsurprisingly, the Mayo Clinic is the top provider to residents. The organization is a world-renowned medical institution employing more than 40,000 people. It has been ranked No. 1 overall in the U.S. News & World Report Best Hospital Rankings for several years. Why the Rochester Area Rocks: Jobs, Opportunity, Quality of Life Top the ListRochester, MN Launches Destination Medical Center Initiative
sections.thingsToDo: Rochester residents never run out of fun things to do. Those who love the arts can't miss the Rochester Art Center and the Rochester Symphony Orchestra & Chorale. The Mayowood Mansion and Plummer House are local historical sites. Residents can also enjoy community gatherings at Thursday's Downtown Summer Market & Music Festival and the Rochester Farmers Market, which showcases local produce and crafts. The Minnesota Children's Museum Rochester and events like Rochesterfest offer engaging experiences for family-friendly outings. Residents can shop at downtown boutiques and enjoy pet-friendly events like Dogs Downtown. Historical sites like the Heritage House Victorian Museum and the History Center of Olmsted County offer insights into the region’s past, while the Rochester Public Library provides access to a wealth of resources and community programs. What is something you can do for free in Rochester, MN? The city hosts several free outdoor concert series with a variety of local live music: The Riverside Concert Series, The Global Music Series, and forWARD Neighborhood Park Concerts. What are some things to do with kids in Rochester, MN? The kids will love learning through play at Spark (The Children’s Museum of Rochester). This interactive space offers hands-on activities for kids to explore and expand their imaginations. Where do locals shop in Rochester, MN? Spend hours perusing the shelves at Garden Party Books, a used bookstore whose owners run Gray Duck Theater & Coffeehouse next door. Kill two birds with one stone and stop in for a great cup of joe. Where is the best place for live music or performing arts in Rochester, MN? The Mayo Civic Center is a multipurpose venue that hosts major events like concerts and live performances throughout the calendar year. Another good choice is Rochester Civic Theatre, a community-based organization that regularly performs plays and musicals. 8 Reasons to Move to Rochester, MNArts, Entertainment, Recreation Enhance Quality of Life in Rochester, MN
sections.outdoors: The area is full of outdoor activities and offers plenty of year-round fun. During the warmer months, residents can stretch their legs on more than 85 miles of paved walking and biking trails, including the Douglas State Trail, or enjoy leisurely trolls and paddle boating at Silver Lake Park. The city has numerous parks, including Quarry Hill Nature Center, providing hiking and nature observation opportunities. Hit the links at any one of the golf courses around the area, including Soldiers Field Golf Course. Water activities like canoeing and kayaking on the Zumbro River and swimming at Foster Arend Beach are also popular. In the winter, the city transforms itself into a snowy playground. Snowshoe or cross-country ski at Quarry Hill Nature Center or ice fish or skate at Rochester-Olmsted Recreation Center. The city’s climate ensures that residents can enjoy outdoor pursuits year-round, from birdwatching and picnicking in the spring and summer to enjoying the colorful foliage in autumn and snowy sports in winter. Why You Should Get Outside Year-Round in Rochester, MN
sections.restaurants: Be ready to eat in Rochester. The city has tons of traditional American fare and blends international cuisines with innovative dining concepts. The Workshop Food Hall provides a unique dining experience with multiple eateries under one roof, including Murdoch’s Place, Birdies, and Julio’s. Locals love spots like Hester’s Kitchen and Bar and Canadian Honker Restaurant & Lounge, offering classic dishes, while international flavors thrive at establishments such as Nupa Mediterranean Cuisine and First Meeting Noodle. Wash it all down with some of the city’s local beverages, including sips from Salem Glen Vineyard and Winery. What is one of the best places to eat in Rochester, MN? Bleu Duck Kitchen, a popular, award-winning Rochester restaurant, offers a Tuesday night date special: two entrees, a bottle of wine and a dessert to share for $65. Looking to get your hands dirty? For something a little more interactive, they also offer fun cooking classes! What is the best coffee shop in Rochester, MN? Hot chocolate, because it gets pretty chilly here in the winter. Locals love this warm, divinely delicious brew at Cafe Steam. Where is the best place for happy hour in Rochester, MN? The craft brew scene is gaining steam in the city. Check out the taprooms of one (or more!) of these five local craft breweries: Little Thistle Brewing, Forager Brewery, Kinney Creek Brewery, LTS Brewing Company and Thesis Beer Project.
sections.education: Residents can take advantage of the city’s strong commitment to education with the presence of the University of Minnesota – Rochester and several other top-ranked public and private schools, which makes it an ideal place for families. More than 17,000 students are enrolled in the Rochester Public School District. There are also more than 20 private schools to pick from. MN-saint-paul.description: Part of the Twin Cities, St. Paul has a vibe that is all its own, full of historic charm, modern amenities and numerous cultural attractions. Top-notch health care facilities, picturesque parks and a thriving economy make it the perfect place to plant roots.

sections.economy: --> --> --> Weather in St. Paul, MN Average Temperatures 55 high / 37 low Average Annual Rainfall 32 in Average Annual Snowfall 52 in

faqs.q: What is the weather like in St. Paul?
faqs.a: St. Paul's winters are cold, but summer and fall are the sweet spot. The average highs and lows are 81°F and 60°F (summer) and 26°F and 10°F (winter).

faqs.q: Does St. Paul get a lot of snow?
faqs.a: The average annual snowfall in St. Paul is roughly 52 inches.

faqs.q: Does St. Paul get a lot of rain?
faqs.a: The average annual rainfall in St. Paul is 32 inches.

faqs.q: How many sunny days does St. Paul get?
faqs.a: St. Paul averages 198 sunny days a year.

faqs.q: What are some things to do outside in St. Paul?
faqs.a: Go on a poetic walk: “Everyday Poems for City Sidewalks” features the works of Saint Paul residents on sidewalk panels. Or, spend the weekend hiking or snowshoeing (depending on the time of year!) at the Mississippi National River & Recreation Area, which spans 72 miles of the Mississippi River.

faqs.q: What is something free to do in St. Paul?
faqs.a: See monkeys, sloths, polar bears, penguins and more animals at the Como Park Zoo and Observatory. Admission is free all year long! You can also appreciate all of Saint Paul's free events and festivals, such as the Saint Paul Art Crawl, the Irish Fair of Minnesota, the Saint Paul Winter Carnival and the Little Mekong Night Market.

faqs.q: What are some fun things to do with kids in St. Paul?
faqs.a: Play putt-putt at Can Can Wonderland, an indoor mini golf course with colorful artist-designed holes, like one designed to look like a grandma’s living room and another that’s an homage to the state fair. This funplex has arcade games and an on-site vintage soda fountain, too.

faqs.q: What is the best open secret about St. Paul?
faqs.a: With 26 miles of riverfront, St. Paul has more shoreline along the Mississippi River than any other city. A favorite spot to take in the river views is Harriet Island Regional Park.

faqs.q: Where do locals shop in St. Paul?
faqs.a: Spend an afternoon browsing the stacks at the Red Balloon Bookshop, a women-owned and -operated bookstore that has served the St. Paul community for more than forty years. The store has a huge selection of children's and adult books, toys, gifts, and several author and illustrator events.

faqs.q: Where is the best place to eat in St. Paul?
faqs.a: Pop into Cossettas for a slice of Italian sausage pizza and gelato, then shop the authentic Italian market for meats and cheeses. The downtown establishment is a St. Paul institution.

faqs.q: Where is the best place for live music or performing arts in St. Paul?
faqs.a: St. Paul is serious about the arts, which is evident in the number of live music and performing arts venues scattered throughout the city. The Palace Theatre is an iconic venue that hosts national touring artists. For a more intimate listening experience, the Turf Club is a neighborhood hang and destination for some of the best touring artists in the country. St. Paul is also home to the Lowertown Arts District, a neighborhood with one of the highest concentrations of artists in the Midwest.

faqs.q: Where is the best happy hour in St. Paul?
faqs.a: Black Hart of St. Paul is an LGBTQ+ soccer bar that hosts drag shows and burlesque. It offers a daily happy hour from 4 to 7 p.m. and free pool and darts after 8 p.m. Sunday through Thursday.

faqs.q: What are the three largest industries in St. Paul?
faqs.a: Health care, finance and retail are major industries in St. Paul. In addition, several major companies have headquarters in the Twin Cities area, including Bremer Financial Corporation, Pearson’s Candy Company and Summit Brewing Company all in St. Paul. Target Corporation, Ameriprise Financial, U.S. Bancorp and Xcel Energy all have a large presence in nearby Minneapolis.

faqs.q: What is the sales tax rate in St. Paul?
faqs.a: In 2024, the sales tax rate in St. Paul is 8.88%.

faqs.q: Does Minnesota have a state income tax?
faqs.a: Yes, Minnesota has a state income tax.

faqs.q: Who are the major health care providers in St. Paul?
faqs.a: The Twin Cities area is home to several world-class hospitals that offer high-quality medical care, like Allina Health United Hospital and M Health Fairview St. John’s Hospital.

faqs.q: What colleges and universities are in St. Paul?
faqs.a: Several smaller colleges and universities are in St. Paul, including Metropolitan State University, Macalester College and the University of St. Thomas.

MN-st-paul.description: Part of the Twin Cities, St. Paul has a vibe that is all its own, full of historic charm, modern amenities and numerous cultural attractions. Top-notch health care facilities, picturesque parks and a thriving economy make it the perfect place to plant roots.

MO-columbia.description: A classic college town, Columbia is home to a welcoming community, excellent schools and a strong economy. Nestled between Kansas City and St. Louis, Columbia boasts a thriving arts scene, lush parks, lively festivals and plenty of small-town charm.

sections.location: Columbia is a lively, college-driven city nestled in the heart of central Missouri, about 125 miles west of St. Louis, easily accessible via Interstate 70. Columbia Regional Airport is 15 miles south, offering flights to Chicago and Dallas, though St. Louis Lambert International Airport, 125 miles east, is the big hub for broader connections. When you’re ready to explore beyond, here’s how close you are to some major nearby cities: St. Louis sits 125 miles east, where you can marvel at the Gateway Arch, wander the Missouri Botanical Garden or catch a Cardinals game. Kansas City is 125 miles west, offering the National WWI Museum, savory barbecue at Gates and Chiefs games at Arrowhead Stadium. Springfield is situated 170 miles southwest, a doable road trip to check out Fantastic Caverns, visit the Wonders of Wildlife National Museum or stroll the historic downtown.
sections.economy: The local economy is firmly rooted in education and educational services, which is the largest industry in town; however, there are plenty of jobs in other sectors, including health care and retail. Unsurprisingly, the University of Missouri, University of Missouri Health Care and Columbia Public Schools are the largest employers here. The university’s presence provides stability and attracts a talented workforce to the area. The cost of living in Columbia is lower than the national average. The median home value in Columbia is $273,349, and the average household income is $98,360. Missouri residents pay a state income tax, and local residents can expect a 7.98% sales tax rate in 2025. Who are the major health care providers in Columbia, MO? Local residents are in good health, thanks to the University of Missouri Health Care’s significant presence. Some major providers are University Hospital, Ellis Fischel Cancer Center and University of Missouri Women’s and Children’s Hospital, all of which are part of the Mizzou system.
sections.thingsToDo: Columbia is a college town, meaning there are many things to do. The District — as the downtown area is known — is a hub of activity and spans 50 square blocks with more than 600 businesses catering to anything and everything you could ever need. The North Village Arts District is packed with artist studios and local shops. For fresh produce, there are three farmers markets in town — Boone County Farmers Market and Columbia Farmers Market are on Saturdays and on Sundays the Orr Street Farmers & Artisans Market takes place. The city also has several antiquing hot spots, including Artichoke Annie’s, Itchy’s Flea Market and the Midway Antique Mall. Art enthusiasts can check out the ever-changing exhibitions at the Columbia Art League and their annual Art in the Park, which is two days of fine arts, crafts, music, food and fun during the first weekend of June each year. Beyond The District, residents can enjoy all the amenities that come with living in a college town and the cultural offerings from the University of Missouri, including the Botanic Garden, Museum of Art and Archaeology, Francis Quadrangle and Mizzou Sports Park. Check out a live performance at The Blue Note, Rose Music Hall or the Missouri Theatre. Families can enjoy classes and coffee at Tiny Town Play Cafe. There are fun festivals throughout the year, too. Some of the favorite festivals around town are True/False Film Fest, Art in the Park and Unbound Book Festival. What is something you can do for free in Columbia, MO? First Fridays in the North Village Arts District is a great way to have free fun here. The monthly event features live music, art exhibits and other performances. What are some things to do in Columbia, MO, with kids? First Chance for Children has a Lend & Learn program that supports children ages 0 to 5 and their families to grow through play. There are two indoor locations where families can enjoy the playroom and activities, all at no cost. Where do locals shop in Columbia, MO? Owned by bestselling author Alex George, downtown’s Skylark Bookshop is a bibliophile’s dream, where the stocked shelves are complemented by a host of events, including monthly book club meetings, evenings with authors and the personalized Skylark Reading Spa. Where is the best place for live music or performing arts in Columbia, MO? The Blue Note is a storied music venue in Columbia that hosts comedians, musicians and other artists.
sections.outdoors: Becoming one with nature is easier than you think in Columbia. The city is home to top trails, like the 9-mile MKT Nature and Fitness Trail, perfect for walking, biking and enjoying the city’s urban landscape. Two state parks — Finger Lakes and Rock Bridge Memorial — provide further opportunities to explore the outdoors. Rock Bridge Memorial features hiking, cycling, cave exploration (including the Devil’s Ice Box!) and plenty of snow-friendly fun, like cross-country skiing. At Finger Lakes, thrill-seekers can enjoy more than 70 miles of off-road ATV trails and a motocross track. For a more relaxed experience, Shelter Gardens offers a tranquil retreat with more than 300 species of trees, 15,000 flowering plants, sculptures, a waterfall and reflecting pool, a rock garden, a gazebo and a sensory garden. Cool off during the summer at Stephens Lake Park and the Albert-Oakland Family Aquatic Center. Watch the sunset over the river at Cooper’s Landing and check out “The Big Tree” — a 350-year-old Bur Oak — at Lewis and Clark National Historic Trail.
sections.restaurants: From casual breweries to upscale experiences, there are plenty of places for foodies to savor the many flavors of Columbia. Craft beer lovers flock to Logboat Brewing for the award-winning brews and rotating food trucks, while 1839 Taphouse has more than two dozen beers on tap. For lively libations, 44 Tavern at Midway Golf and Games combines food and fun, and The Roof at the Broadway Hotel serves up stunning views with specialty cocktails. Wine enthusiasts can sample local wines at The Tasting Room on The Blufftop at Rocheport. Beyond the breweries, the city has several upscale restaurants, like CC’s City Broiler, Sophias and Sycamore. Mahi’s Ethiopian Kitchen, Pho Quan Viet and Burmese food from Myanmar Kitchen can’t be missed for those seeking international flavors. Cap off a meal with dessert from Sparky’s Homemade Ice Cream. Finally, if you’re looking for a unique way to savor the flavors of Columbia, recruit the help of Columbia Culinary Tours, which provides a fun way to experience the downtown food scene and offers a variety of experiences, including a dessert tour. What is one of the best places to eat in Columbia, MO? Oh, wherefore art thou, pizza delivery from Shakespeare’s Pizza? When here, you must order the Masterpiece from Shakespeare’s, a Mizzou institution: red onions, pepperoni, Canadian bacon, mushrooms, black olives, green peppers, Italian sausage and ground beef. Looking for a truly special spot? The Barred Owl Butcher & Table is a restaurant, bar and butcher that features seasonal and local ingredients. What is the best coffee shop in Columbia, MO? Uprise Bakery is a bakery-meets-bar that functions as a coffeehouse during the day and a watering hole at night. Where is the best happy hour in Columbia, MO? The Heidelberg (or the ‘Berg as it’s known to locals) is a classic tavern that’s been a happy hour institution since it opened in 1963. Located in The District, On the Rocks is famous for low-priced (but delicious) martini options, signature cocktails and jumbo shots.
sections.education: With over 20,000 students, you can count on a dynamic, well-educated vibe coursing through Columbia. Naturally, the city places a significant emphasis on the importance of education, which is reflected in the variety of educational options available. Residents can enjoy a mix of public, private and charter schools. The Columbia School District has a population of nearly 19,000 students and is among the best districts in Missouri. Additionally, the district has a strong gifted education program with a dedicated center for gifted students. There are many religious and independent private schools for families to pick from, too, often with a long history of high-quality education and smaller class sizes. Columbia Independent School is renowned for its strong focus on academic excellence and college preparation. faqs.a: The Heidelberg (or the ‘Berg as it’s known to locals) is a classic tavern that’s been a happy hour institution since it opened in 1963. Located in The District, On the Rocks is famous for low-priced (but delicious) martini options, signature cocktails and jumbo shots.

faqs.a: There are several higher-ed institutions located here, including the University of Missouri, Columbia College and Stephens College.

MO-saint-louis.description: From the iconic Gateway Arch to the beloved Cardinals, St. Louis has a one-of-a-kind community with renowned museums, diverse neighborhoods and a dynamic economy, all at an affordable price.

sections.location: St. Louis is a vibrant city on the eastern border of Missouri, about 250 miles east of Kansas City, accessible via Interstates 70 and 64. St. Louis Lambert International Airport is just northwest, offering worldwide flights. Nearby major cities include: Springfield, MO, sits 215 miles southwest, boasting the Fantastic Caverns, Wonders of Wildlife National Museum and Dickerson Park Zoo. Chicago, IL, is 300 miles northeast, where you can cruise the river to admire architecture, visit the Art Institute or root for the Cubs at Wrigley Field. Memphis, TN, is roughly 280 miles south, buzzing with the excitement of Beale Street and home to attractions like Graceland and the National Civil Rights Museum.
sections.economy: St. Louis has established itself as a career leader in health sciences and services, though the area has a strong tradition of leadership in transportation and logistics. It’s also an emerging hot spot for financial services and investment advisors. In recent years, the city has carved out a reputation as a tech hot spot, drawing tech companies, remote workers and entrepreneurs eager to join (and grow) the innovative, creative tech scene here. Some of the largest employers here are Washington University, BJC HealthCare and Boeing. Edward Jones Investments, a financial firm, is headquartered in St. Louis. The cost of living in St. Louis is slightly lower than the national average. The median home value in St. Louis is $197,774, and the average household income is $87,663. Missouri residents pay a state income tax, and local residents can expect a 9.68% sales tax rate in 2025. Who are the major health care providers in St. Louis, MO? St. Louis is home to Barnes-Jewish Hospital, one of the most highly rated hospitals in the country, according to U.S. News & World Report. In addition to having one of the nation’s best obstetrics and gynecology programs, Barnes-Jewish ranks highly in 10 other adult specialties, high-performing in one specialty and high-performing in 20 different procedures and conditions. Other top providers include St. Louis Children’s Hospital, Mercy Hospital-St. Lewis and SSM Health Saint Louis University Hospital. 11 Reasons to Start a Business in St. Louis Right Now
sections.thingsToDo: Locals in St. Louis will never run out of things to do. The city has a thriving arts scene, with free admission to several spots, including the Saint Louis Art Museum and the Saint Louis Zoo, as well as unique venues like the City Museum and the Pulitzer Arts Foundation. When it comes to music and live performances, the Fabulous Fox Theatre, Powell Hall (home of the St. Louis Symphony Orchestra) and The Pageant are can’t-miss spots. Sports lovers can root, root, root for the home team by cheering on the St. Louis Cardinals at Busch Stadium and the St. Louis Blues at Enterprise Center to experience the city’s sports culture. Beyond its arts and sports scenes, St. Louis residents can appreciate the iconic Gateway Arch, a 630-foot monument. The St. Louis Union Station was once one of the largest and busiest passenger rail terminals, and today, it is a popular entertainment spot. Residents can also explore the city's history through tours of the Anheuser-Busch brewery and visits to historic sites, and its eclectic neighborhoods offer a wide range of dining and shopping experiences. What is something you can do for free in St. Louis, MO? The city has more free major tourist attractions than any other city in the U.S. outside of Washington, D.C., so there are plenty of free things to do to pick from. Entry is free to the Saint Louis Zoo, home to 16,000 animals, but do bring some money to ride the carousel. What are some things to do in St. Louis, MO, with kids? Don’t be surprised if you leave and your child’s new favorite subject is science. The Saint Louis Science Center has all kinds of excellent exhibits and attractions, like a virtual reality transporter and more than 100,000 “artifacts of wonder.” Where do locals shop in St. Louis, MO? Artisans in THE LOOP is a boutique gallery with art pieces from more than 80 local and national vendors. Stock up on a variety of local products, from spices to baked goods, produce and meats, at the Soulard Farmers Market. Where is the best place for live music or performing arts in St. Louis, MO? Chaifetz Arena at St. Louis University attracts talent from all over the country and is a favorite spot for big names and artists. Check out live music at Broadway Oyster Bar for a more intimate setting. 8 Things To Do in St. Louis8 Reasons To Move to St. Louis, MO
sections.outdoors: Stretch your legs in St. Louis! With more than 100 parks that provide plentiful recreational opportunities, including pools, skating rinks and sports courts, there are several spots for residents to get out and about. Forest Park is larger than Central Park and offers 30 miles of trails, golf courses and the Emerson Grand Basin, perfect for canoeing, all alongside attractions like the Muny Amphitheater and the St. Louis Zoo. Citygarden and Laumeier Sculpture Park blend art and nature, featuring sculptures in green spaces. Residents can also explore the Mississippi via riverboat cruises or enjoy seasonal activities like sledding at Art Hill. Beyond the parks, residents can explore numerous trails — more than 607 miles of them! — and waterways are great for hiking, biking and paddling. Tower Grove Park is a designated birding trail with meadows, pavilions and a farmers market. It also hosts the annual Festival of Nations, celebrating the city’s numerous cultures. Additionally, attractions like the Audubon Center at Riverlands and Lone Elk Park provide wildlife viewing and nature exploration opportunities.
sections.restaurants: You’ll never go hungry here. The city is rapidly gaining recognition as a culinary destination and offers an array of dining experiences, from traditional St. Louis specialties like toasted ravioli to innovative international cuisines found along South Grand Boulevard and in neighborhoods like The Hill. Residents can enjoy everything from Turkish kebabs and Ethiopian injera to Vietnamese pho and Bosnian dishes, reflecting the city’s multicultural makeup. The Delmar Loop is one of the most popular dining districts. It got its name from a former streetcar loop in the area, but now it's home to more than 154 restaurants, specialty shops, entertainment venues and art galleries.The beverage scene is equally exciting. The city is home to the original Anheuser-Busch Brewery, offering tours and tastings of its famous brews. That innovation spills over into the St. Louis craft beer scene, with 65 local breweries that include favorites like Schlafly. What is one of the best places to eat in St. Louis, MO? Eleven Eleven Mississippi is a St. Louis staple, serving up rustic Tuscan cuisine. After dinner, head over to Baileys’ Chocolate Bar, which has a “Lover’s Plate” sampling of signature desserts like strawberry kisses and bourbon caramel corn banana brûlée. What is the best coffee shop in St. Louis, MO? Sump Coffee features a broad selection of coffee, teas and quick bites. Where is the best happy hour in St. Louis, MO? Planter’s House is the perfect happy hour spot, serving up some of the best cocktails in the city and a beautiful atmosphere. And when you can’t stay, they also have to-go cocktails available in 16-ounce bottles. Living the Dream: I Left My Corporate Job and Opened an Ice Cream Shop
sections.education: St. Louis places a significant emphasis on the importance of education, which is reflected in the variety of educational options, with a mix of public, private and charter schools. Several public school districts are in the area, but Saint Louis Public Schools (SLPS) is the largest, with more than 18,000 students enrolled. SLPS offers several charter and magnet schools with specialized themes, including STEM, arts and language immersion. Though performance varies from school to school, the district maintains a favorable student-to-teacher ratio of 14:1. Regarding private education, the city has a large presence of religious and independent private schools, often with a long history of high-quality education and smaller class sizes. St. Louis has several outstanding options — according to Niche, four of the five best private schools in Missouri are located here. John Burroughs School, MICDS – Mary Institute & St. Louis Country Day School, Thomas Jefferson School and The Whitfield School are among the most highly regarded institutions in the state. --> --> --> faqs.q: Who are the major health care providers in St. Louis, MO?
faqs.a: St. Louis is home to Barnes-Jewish Hospital, one of the most highly rated hospitals in the country, according to U.S. News & World Report. In addition to having one of the nation’s best obstetrics and gynecology programs, Barnes-Jewish ranks highly in 10 other adult specialties, high-performing in one specialty and high-performing in 20 different procedures and conditions. Other top providers include St. Louis Children’s Hospital, Mercy Hospital-St. Lewis and SSM Health Saint Louis University Hospital.

faqs.q: What is something you can do for free in St. Louis, MO?
faqs.a: The city has more free major tourist attractions than any other city in the U.S. outside of Washington, D.C., so there are plenty of free things to do to pick from. Entry is free to the Saint Louis Zoo, home to 16,000 animals, but do bring some money to ride the carousel.

faqs.q: What are some things to do in St. Louis, MO, with kids?
faqs.a: Don’t be surprised if you leave and your child’s new favorite subject is science. The Saint Louis Science Center has all kinds of excellent exhibits and attractions, like a virtual reality transporter and more than 100,000 “artifacts of wonder.”

faqs.q: Where do locals shop in St. Louis, MO?
faqs.a: Artisans in THE LOOP is a boutique gallery with art pieces from more than 80 local and national vendors. Stock up on a variety of local products, from spices to baked goods, produce and meats, at the Soulard Farmers Market.

faqs.q: Where is the best place for live music or performing arts in St. Louis, MO?
faqs.a: Chaifetz Arena at St. Louis University attracts talent from all over the country and is a favorite spot for big names and artists. Check out live music at Broadway Oyster Bar for a more intimate setting.

faqs.q: What is one of the best places to eat in St. Louis, MO?
faqs.a: Eleven Eleven Mississippi is a St. Louis staple, serving up rustic Tuscan cuisine. After dinner, head over to Baileys’ Chocolate Bar, which has a “Lover’s Plate” sampling of signature desserts like strawberry kisses and bourbon caramel corn banana brûlée.

faqs.q: What is the best coffee shop in St. Louis, MO?
faqs.a: Sump Coffee features a broad selection of coffee, teas and quick bites.

faqs.q: Where is the best happy hour in St. Louis, MO?
faqs.a: Planter’s House is the perfect happy hour spot, serving up some of the best cocktails in the city and a beautiful atmosphere. And when you can’t stay, they also have to-go cocktails available in 16-ounce bottles.

faqs.q: What colleges and universities are in St. Louis, MO?
faqs.a: The city has several colleges and universities, including Washington University, Saint Louis University and the University of Missouri-St. Louis.

MO-st-louis.description: From the iconic Gateway Arch to the beloved Cardinals, St. Louis has a one-of-a-kind community with renowned museums, diverse neighborhoods and a dynamic economy, all at an affordable price.

sections.location: St. Louis is a vibrant city on the eastern border of Missouri, about 250 miles east of Kansas City, accessible via Interstates 70 and 64. St. Louis Lambert International Airport is just northwest, offering worldwide flights. Nearby major cities include: Springfield, MO, sits 215 miles southwest, boasting the Fantastic Caverns, Wonders of Wildlife National Museum and Dickerson Park Zoo. Chicago, IL, is 300 miles northeast, where you can cruise the river to admire architecture, visit the Art Institute or root for the Cubs at Wrigley Field. Memphis, TN, is roughly 280 miles south, buzzing with the excitement of Beale Street and home to attractions like Graceland and the National Civil Rights Museum.
sections.economy: St. Louis has established itself as a career leader in health sciences and services, though the area has a strong tradition of leadership in transportation and logistics. It’s also an emerging hot spot for financial services and investment advisors. In recent years, the city has carved out a reputation as a tech hot spot, drawing tech companies, remote workers and entrepreneurs eager to join (and grow) the innovative, creative tech scene here. Some of the largest employers here are Washington University, BJC HealthCare and Boeing. Edward Jones Investments, a financial firm, is headquartered in St. Louis. The cost of living in St. Louis is slightly lower than the national average. The median home value in St. Louis is $197,774, and the average household income is $87,663. Missouri residents pay a state income tax, and local residents can expect a 9.68% sales tax rate in 2025. Who are the major health care providers in St. Louis, MO? St. Louis is home to Barnes-Jewish Hospital, one of the most highly rated hospitals in the country, according to U.S. News & World Report. In addition to having one of the nation’s best obstetrics and gynecology programs, Barnes-Jewish ranks highly in 10 other adult specialties, high-performing in one specialty and high-performing in 20 different procedures and conditions. Other top providers include St. Louis Children’s Hospital, Mercy Hospital-St. Lewis and SSM Health Saint Louis University Hospital. 11 Reasons to Start a Business in St. Louis Right Now
sections.thingsToDo: Locals in St. Louis will never run out of things to do. The city has a thriving arts scene, with free admission to several spots, including the Saint Louis Art Museum and the Saint Louis Zoo, as well as unique venues like the City Museum and the Pulitzer Arts Foundation. When it comes to music and live performances, the Fabulous Fox Theatre, Powell Hall (home of the St. Louis Symphony Orchestra) and The Pageant are can’t-miss spots. Sports lovers can root, root, root for the home team by cheering on the St. Louis Cardinals at Busch Stadium and the St. Louis Blues at Enterprise Center to experience the city’s sports culture. Beyond its arts and sports scenes, St. Louis residents can appreciate the iconic Gateway Arch, a 630-foot monument. The St. Louis Union Station was once one of the largest and busiest passenger rail terminals, and today, it is a popular entertainment spot. Residents can also explore the city's history through tours of the Anheuser-Busch brewery and visits to historic sites, and its eclectic neighborhoods offer a wide range of dining and shopping experiences. What is something you can do for free in St. Louis, MO? The city has more free major tourist attractions than any other city in the U.S. outside of Washington, D.C., so there are plenty of free things to do to pick from. Entry is free to the Saint Louis Zoo, home to 16,000 animals, but do bring some money to ride the carousel. What are some things to do in St. Louis, MO, with kids? Don’t be surprised if you leave and your child’s new favorite subject is science. The Saint Louis Science Center has all kinds of excellent exhibits and attractions, like a virtual reality transporter and more than 100,000 “artifacts of wonder.” Where do locals shop in St. Louis, MO? Artisans in THE LOOP is a boutique gallery with art pieces from more than 80 local and national vendors. Stock up on a variety of local products, from spices to baked goods, produce and meats, at the Soulard Farmers Market. Where is the best place for live music or performing arts in St. Louis, MO? Chaifetz Arena at St. Louis University attracts talent from all over the country and is a favorite spot for big names and artists. Check out live music at Broadway Oyster Bar for a more intimate setting. 8 Things To Do in St. Louis8 Reasons To Move to St. Louis, MO
sections.outdoors: Stretch your legs in St. Louis! With more than 100 parks that provide plentiful recreational opportunities, including pools, skating rinks and sports courts, there are several spots for residents to get out and about. Forest Park is larger than Central Park and offers 30 miles of trails, golf courses and the Emerson Grand Basin, perfect for canoeing, all alongside attractions like the Muny Amphitheater and the St. Louis Zoo. Citygarden and Laumeier Sculpture Park blend art and nature, featuring sculptures in green spaces. Residents can also explore the Mississippi via riverboat cruises or enjoy seasonal activities like sledding at Art Hill. Beyond the parks, residents can explore numerous trails — more than 607 miles of them! — and waterways are great for hiking, biking and paddling. Tower Grove Park is a designated birding trail with meadows, pavilions and a farmers market. It also hosts the annual Festival of Nations, celebrating the city’s numerous cultures. Additionally, attractions like the Audubon Center at Riverlands and Lone Elk Park provide wildlife viewing and nature exploration opportunities.
sections.restaurants: You’ll never go hungry here. The city is rapidly gaining recognition as a culinary destination and offers an array of dining experiences, from traditional St. Louis specialties like toasted ravioli to innovative international cuisines found along South Grand Boulevard and in neighborhoods like The Hill. Residents can enjoy everything from Turkish kebabs and Ethiopian injera to Vietnamese pho and Bosnian dishes, reflecting the city’s multicultural makeup. The Delmar Loop is one of the most popular dining districts. It got its name from a former streetcar loop in the area, but now it's home to more than 154 restaurants, specialty shops, entertainment venues and art galleries.The beverage scene is equally exciting. The city is home to the original Anheuser-Busch Brewery, offering tours and tastings of its famous brews. That innovation spills over into the St. Louis craft beer scene, with 65 local breweries that include favorites like Schlafly. What is one of the best places to eat in St. Louis, MO? Eleven Eleven Mississippi is a St. Louis staple, serving up rustic Tuscan cuisine. After dinner, head over to Baileys’ Chocolate Bar, which has a “Lover’s Plate” sampling of signature desserts like strawberry kisses and bourbon caramel corn banana brûlée. What is the best coffee shop in St. Louis, MO? Sump Coffee features a broad selection of coffee, teas and quick bites. Where is the best happy hour in St. Louis, MO? Planter’s House is the perfect happy hour spot, serving up some of the best cocktails in the city and a beautiful atmosphere. And when you can’t stay, they also have to-go cocktails available in 16-ounce bottles. Living the Dream: I Left My Corporate Job and Opened an Ice Cream Shop
sections.education: St. Louis places a significant emphasis on the importance of education, which is reflected in the variety of educational options, with a mix of public, private and charter schools. Several public school districts are in the area, but Saint Louis Public Schools (SLPS) is the largest, with more than 18,000 students enrolled. SLPS offers several charter and magnet schools with specialized themes, including STEM, arts and language immersion. Though performance varies from school to school, the district maintains a favorable student-to-teacher ratio of 14:1. Regarding private education, the city has a large presence of religious and independent private schools, often with a long history of high-quality education and smaller class sizes. St. Louis has several outstanding options — according to Niche, four of the five best private schools in Missouri are located here. John Burroughs School, MICDS – Mary Institute & St. Louis Country Day School, Thomas Jefferson School and The Whitfield School are among the most highly regarded institutions in the state. --> --> --> MT-billings.description: Stunning scenery and nearby Yellowstone National Park are just two reasons Billings, MT, is a perfect place for nature lovers. With friendly locals, top-notch schools and a low cost of living, residents enjoy a laid-back lifestyle and the beauty of living in the heart of Big Sky Country.

sections.location: Billings is a rugged, wide-open city perched in southern Montana, about 140 miles east of Bozeman, where Interstate 90 slices through the high plains like a lifeline to the Big Sky Country. Billings Logan International Airport is right in the city, a convenient hub offering flights to Denver, Salt Lake City and beyond. When you’re ready to explore, here’s how close you are to some major nearby cities: Bozeman is 140 miles west, where you can scope out the Museum of the Rockies, ski at Bridger Bowl or wander the vibrant Main Street. Missoula lies 340 miles west, boasting the University of Montana, Mount Sentinel and some of the Clark Fork River’s scenic trails. Great Falls is roughly 220 miles northwest, with the C.M. Russell Museum, Giant Springs State Park and the River’s Edge Trail.
sections.economy: Billings’ economy is deeply rooted in a variety of industries, and the city is a key economic center in Montana. The largest industries in the area are health care, retail and accommodation and food services, thanks to the large number of tourists who visit the city each year. Some significant employers in Billings include the Billings Clinic, St. Vincent Healthcare and Billings Public Schools. Also, First Interstate BancSystem, a bank holding company for regional bank First Interstate Bank, is headquartered within the city. The cost of living in Billings is slightly higher than the national average. The median home value in Billings is $341,146, and the average household income is $107,569. Montana residents pay a state income tax. And because Montana doesn’t have a state sales tax, no local sales taxes are applied in Billings. Who are the major health care providers in Billings, MT? The Billings Clinic was named the best hospital in Southeast Montana by U.S. News & World Report. This leading provider offers a comprehensive range of services and is well-known for its cancer center. Other providers include Advanced Care Hospital of Montana and Intermountain Health St. Vincent Regional Hospital.
sections.thingsToDo: Billings seamlessly blends culture with family-friendly attractions set against the open Montana sky. The downtown area is a hub for artistic exploration, featuring the Alberta Bair Theater for live performances, the Billings Public Library for community events and a thriving arts scene highlighted during ArtWalk every other month. Museums like the Western Heritage Center and the Yellowstone Art Museum provide insights into the region’s history and art, while the Moss Mansion offers a glimpse into historic architecture. Families can enjoy attractions like ZooMontana, the Reef Indoor Water Park and the hands-on Wise Wonders Science & Discover Museum. Several community events like the Strawberry Festival add to Billings’ community-forward reputation. Local residents are also very wellness-minded. Studios like Limber Tree Yoga offer unique experiences for those seeking rest and relaxation, including aerial yoga and rooftop sessions with mountain views. What is something you can do for free in Billings, MT? Spend an afternoon picnicking, playing volleyball, or just watching the geese and ducks at Riverfront Park, a lovely spot along the Yellowstone River. What are some things to do in Billings, MT, with kids? Kids can learn to climb and attend summer camps at Steepworld Climbing and Fitness. Where do locals shop in Billings, MT? The inventory at Liberty & Vine Country Store focuses on Montana-made goods, and there are also all kinds of unique treasures in the antique warehouse. Where is the best place for live music or performing arts in Billings, MT? See plays, musicals and improv comedy at Nova Center for the Performing Arts.
sections.outdoors: Billings has many ways to explore the great outdoors, from leisurely park visits to adventurous explorations. Riverfront Park is the perfect place to relax by the Yellowstone River, and Pioneer Park is ideal for sports and picnics. Bike at Zimmerman Park or Swords Park, and the scenic Four Dances Recreation Area also offers views of the city and mountains. The Yellowstone Wildlife Sanctuary provides an opportunity to see rescued native animals, while the botanical DanWalt Gardens offers a peaceful stroll.Just beyond the city limits, outdoor adventures abound. Pictograph Cave State Park offers archaeological exploration, and the Beartooth Highway is an excellent spot for a scenic drive. Lake Elmo State Park offers family-friendly recreation, and the Rimrocks provide a place to rock climb and rappel. Historical sites like Pompey’s Pillar National Monument transport visitors back in time to the Lewis and Clark expedition and Native American History.
sections.restaurants: Bite into Billings restaurant scene. The city is known for its walkable Billings Brew Trail, which features a number of breweries, distilleries and a cider house and showcases the region’s craft beer scene. Many of the stops are along a 1.5-mile loop through the historic downtown area, so you can make it a DIY pub crawl adventure. The Sassy Biscuit is a local staple, and establishments like Walker’s Grill, under the guidance of James Beard-nominated Chef Nick Steen Gullings, provide innovative dishes with seasonal ingredients, emphasizing a farm-to-table movement. Other upscale favorites include TEN at the Northern Hotel. The city also embraces international flavors and farm-to-table concepts, with restaurants highlighting local produce and meats. After dinner, indulge in handcrafted ice cream at Big Dipper Ice Cream, and cocktail lovers can grab a drink from Doc Harper’s Tavern. What is one of the best places to eat in Billings, MT? Try unique burgers from The Burger Dive, like the award-winning “I’m Your Huckleberry” burger with huckleberry hatch chili barbecue sauce, bacon, goat cheese, arugula and roasted red pepper mayo. The spot has an old-school diner vibe, and you’ll also want to save room for a milkshake, like the oatmeal cream pie, the flaming s’mores or the cinnamon bear. What is the best coffee shop in Billings, MT? Maple Moose Coffee and Pop Shop specializes in coffee and ice cream. What more do you need? Where is the best happy hour in Billings, MT? Get $4 beers from 4 to 6 p.m. Monday through Friday at Montana Brewing Company, which opened in 1994 and was the state’s first brewpub! Everything from IPAs to stouts to seasonal fruit wheats are on tap, in addition to wine, cocktails and a solid selection of mules, plus a nonalcoholic root beer float.
sections.education: Billings is generally considered family-friendly because of its ever-growing list of amenities, services and activities that cater to growing families. You can find a diverse variety of public and private schools, as well as alternative education options and higher-ed institutions. The Billings Public School District is one of the largest in the state. It offers a range of programs and schools, including traditional classrooms, alternative schools and focus programs, like magnet schools with specialized themes. The city also has many religious and independent private schools, often with a long history of high-quality education and smaller class sizes. MT-missoula.description: Under the Big Sky of Montana is Missoula, a city with unparalleled natural beauty. Missoula’s endless outdoor activities, friendly locals, renowned breweries and a strong sense of environmental stewardship cultivate a tight-knit community.

sections.economy: --> --> --> Weather in Missoula, MT Average Temperatures 58 high / 34 low Average Annual Rainfall 16 in Average Annual Snowfall 45 in

faqs.q: What is the weather like in Missoula?
faqs.a: Missoula has four distinct seasons, with river tubing popular in the summer months and skiing and boarding at Snowbowl in the winter. The average highs and lows are 81°F and 50°F (summer) and 36°F and 20°F (winter).

faqs.q: Does Missoula get a lot of snow?
faqs.a: The average annual snowfall in Missoula is 45 inches a year, with December being the snowiest month on average.

faqs.q: Does Missoula get a lot of rain?
faqs.a: The average annual rainfall in Missoula is below the national average at just 16 inches a year.

faqs.q: How many sunny days does Missoula get?
faqs.a: Missoula averages 152 sunny days a year.

faqs.q: What are some things to do outside in Missoula?
faqs.a: Thirteen hot springs are within a two-hour reach of Missoula, making for a relaxing getaway. But if you’re looking to stay closer to home, grab your four-legged friends and head to Jacobs Island Bark Park, a 6-acre dog park along the Clark Fork River. Be sure to take a spin on the historic and majestic carousel and check out nearby Caras Park.

faqs.q: What is something free to do in Missoula?
faqs.a: Slip away to the forest and take a hike (or go snowshoeing in the winter) at Rattlesnake National Recreation Area & Wilderness.

faqs.q: What are some fun things to do with kids in Missoula?
faqs.a: Visit the Spectrum Discovery Area for its hands-on science exhibits. Or take the family to Dragon Hollow, an absolutely massive inclusive play area with a three-headed dragon. On certain days, the nearby carousel is free for kids to ride, too.

faqs.q: What is the best open secret about Missoula?
faqs.a: Missoula is all about the outdoors. The city is home to an extensive system of urban trails that wind throughout the city and surrounding areas. Riverfront Trail and Mount Jumbo Trail are two favorites among locals.

faqs.q: Where do locals shop in Missoula?
faqs.a: For the bookworm, pay a visit to Fact & Fiction Bookstore, an indie bookstore with a wide selection of titles that spotlight many Montana authors. Downtown is also filled with local boutiques and galleries, making it a fun place to explore for unique items.

faqs.q: What is a fun place for dinner in Missoula?
faqs.a: The Notorious P.I.G. is a popular barbecue restaurant known for its smoked meat and downright delectable menu crafted using techniques and recipes from St. Louis pitmasters. Many residents enjoy this spot's casual but lively atmosphere.

faqs.q: Where is the best place for live music or performing arts in Missoula?
faqs.a: Looking for a great night out on the town? The Wilma is a historic theatre on Higgins built in 1921 and dedicated to opera artist Edna Wilma. It's a great place to catch movies, film festivals, and a slew of other concerts and shows.

faqs.q: Where is the best happy hour in Missoula?
faqs.a: Happy hour starts at 4:06 on the dot at Stave & Hoop, a speakeasy with burgers and classic cocktails. (BTW, 406 is the area code in Missoula).

faqs.q: What are the three largest industries in Missoula?
faqs.a: Missoula has a diverse economy with several major industries contributing to the local economy, including health care, education and retail. The largest employers are the University of Montana, Providence St. Patrick Hospital and Missoula County Public Schools.

faqs.q: What is the sales tax rate in Missoula?
faqs.a: Montana does not have a state sales tax, and no local sales taxes apply in Missoula.

faqs.q: Does Montana have a state income tax?
faqs.a: Yes, Montana has a state income tax.

faqs.q: Who are the major health care providers in Missoula?
faqs.a: Providence St. Patrick Hospital keeps Missoula residents in good health. According to U.S. News & World Report, the hospital performs highly in two adult specialties and 13 procedures and conditions.

faqs.q: What colleges and universities are in Missoula?
faqs.a: There are several colleges and universities in Missoula, including the University of Montana.

NC-asheville.description: Tucked away in the Blue Ridge Mountains, Asheville enchants residents with its blend of natural beauty and sense of community. Home to a thriving food and craft beer scene, diverse economic opportunities and easy access to endless outdoor activities, Asheville offers a unique lifestyle that celebrates creativity and community.

sections.location: Asheville is a vibrant city in the Blue Ridge Mountains of western North Carolina, right along Interstate 26 and Interstate 40. Asheville Regional Airport is about 15 miles south, making travel easy. When you’re ready to explore, here’s how close you are to some major nearby cities: Charlotte, NC, is just 130 miles east, where you can visit the Levine Museum of the New South or the U.S. National Whitewater Center. Knoxville, TN, sits 115 miles west, offering the Knoxville Museum of Art and the World’s Fair Park. Greenville, SC, is about 60 miles south, home to Falls Park on the Reedy and the Peace Center.
sections.economy: The city has a dynamic economy with several key industries, including health care, tourism and manufacturing. Some notable employers are Mission Health System, Buncombe County Schools and The Biltmore Company. The local economy is characterized by its focus on tourism, and returning to the mountains is the best way to help the city recover. The cost of living in Asheville is slightly lower than the national average. The median home value in Asheville is $407,100, and the average household income is $103,704. North Carolina residents pay a state income tax, and local residents can expect a 7.0% sales tax rate in 2025. Who are the major health care providers in Asheville, NC? Mission Hospital is the largest hospital in the region and is nationally recognized for its quality of care. The hospital is also highly rated in several specialties and procedures, including cardiology, orthopedics and urology. Asheville, NC: Where Entrepreneurs ExcelAsheville, NC, Helps Residents Stay in Good HealthEntrepreneurial Spirit Is Alive in Asheville, NC
sections.thingsToDo: The community has a cool, artsy vibe that mixes culture and outdoor fun. Downtown packs galleries, shops and live music, ranging from small shows to larger festivals, like the annual Goombay Festival. Check out spots like the Biltmore Estate, Folk Art Center and River Arts District, where artists open their studios. The city celebrates its unique culture with events like Hola, a festival highlighting Latin culture, and Goombay to celebrate the African diaspora and Asheville’s African American community. The Wortham Center has everything from smaller crafts to modern pieces. Downtown’s walkable streets buzz with excitement, and residents often see performers and drum circles. Historical spots like Thomas Wolfe’s childhood home and quirky stops like the Pinball Museum add to the city’s eclectic vibe. Outdoor enthusiasts can find tranquility through forest bathing tours, or shop until you drop at the local boutiques and vintage stores on Lexington Avenue and in Biltmore Village. What is something you can do for free in Asheville, NC? Dance to the beats of the Asheville Drum Circle, an informal gathering on Friday evenings in downtown Pritchard Park. What are some things to do in Asheville, NC, with kids? The Asheville Museum of Science captivates budding minds with its exhibits showcasing all the wonders of the natural world. Outdoor enthusiasts often rave over the North Carolina Arboretum, a hot spot where families can explore themed gardens and dive into all kinds of educational programs. The WNC Nature Center is another popular place with a reputation for teaching visitors about the area’s abundant native wildlife. Where do locals shop in Asheville, NC? Lexington Avenue has boutiques, vintage shops, hemp stores and more. Be sure to pop into Lexington Glassworks to watch glass-blowing magic and shop colorful housewares. Then, end your afternoon by walking to The Chocolate Fetish for a sweet treat. The chocolate shop features award-winning, handmade chocolates and truffles. Where is the best place for live music or performing arts in Asheville, NC? The city is known throughout the region for its phenomenal arts and music scene. One of the standout venues for live music is The Orange Peel. Located in the heart of downtown Asheville, this iconic venue has earned quite the reputation as one of the best places to catch a show. The venue has hosted iconic artists like Bob Dylan and Joan Jett, as well as local and up-and-coming artists. 5 Can’t-Miss Festivals in Asheville, NCAsheville’s Arts Scene Has Deep Roots and a Bright FutureAsheville, NC, Offers Something Special for Everyone
sections.outdoors: The local outdoor scene is basically a choose-your-own-adventure for nature lovers. Asheville is surrounded by lush mountains and forests, with the French Broad River adding to its charm. Looking for something peaceful? Walk through the Botanical Gardens at Asheville or paddle the French Broad River. Feeling adventurous? Whitewater raft the Green River or hit the Bent Creek Experimental Forest mountain biking trails. Hike Pisgah and Nantahala national forests, hit the Mountains-to-Sea Trail or drive along the Blue Ridge Parkway for those iconic views. Don’t miss Craggy Gardens’ flower power. Richmond Hill Park and NC Arboretum add even more easy-accessible trails and natural experiences. Where Locals Go to Hike, Bike and Paddle in AshevilleOutdoor Activities in Asheville Give Residents a Natural HighAsk an Expert: Asheville’s Can’t-Miss Hikes
sections.restaurants: You’ll never go hungry in Asheville. In fact, the city is a designated Foodtopian Society, referring to utopian experiments that use food to create a more just and equal world. The city certainly lives up to the hype. Think farm-to-table eats, where chefs turn local bounties into culinary masterpieces. Want to dig deeper? Food tours and foraging trips are a thing here. Hit up local farmers markets and the WNC Cheese Trail for regional bites. And let’s talk drinks: the local craft beer scene is massive. Asheville holds the title for the second-highest number of breweries per capita in the country, with 17 breweries for every 50,000 people. With more than 50 breweries across town, including favorites like Asheville Brewing Company and Highland Brewing, and a killer distillery scene, you’ll never go thirsty. What is one of the best places to eat in Asheville, NC? At Chai Pani, share nibbles and snacks reminiscent of those served by street vendors throughout India. Chai Pani even won a coveted James Beard “Outstanding Restaurant” award. What is the best coffee shop in Asheville, NC? When you can’t decide between a happy hour or a caffeinated pick-me-up, head to The Times Bar and Coffee Shop, which serves classic cocktails, craft coffee and boozy caffeinated drinks. Battery Park Book Exchange and Champagne Bar is the perfect union of books and bubbly. Guests can look through a huge selection of used books, enjoy live music and sip champagne all the while. Where is the best happy hour in Asheville, NC? Rye Knot is a brewery and distillery that makes for a perfect post-work gathering space. You can even take home a cut of meat from the curing cabinet and make a tasty steak at home. Asheville Food Trucks (and Trikes!) Deliver Satisfaction4 Kid-Friendly Breweries in Asheville
sections.education: Creativity and collaboration come alive through the city's schools. The city is primarily served by the Asheville City Schools and Buncombe County Schools districts, each with a favorable student-to-teacher ratio, which is desirable for families looking for more individualized attention. Local schools are also committed to diversity, equity and inclusion, creating a welcoming environment for all students. Browse the Top 100 Best Places to Live in the U.S. Asheville, NC 699 Aurora, CO 699 Gresham, OR 700 Madison, WI 700 Omaha, NE 701 Appleton, WI 704 Frederick, MD 704 Sioux Falls, SD 704 Fargo, ND 706 Lincoln, NE 707 Iowa City, IA 709 Durham, NC 710 Greenville, SC 710 College Station, TX 711 Norman, OK 711 Midland, TX 712 Ann Arbor, MI 715 Rio Rancho, NM 716 Coral Springs, FL 717 Cedar Rapids, IA 718 Charleston, SC 719 Rochester, MN 722 Bloomington, IL 727 Pittsburgh, PA 727 Nashua, NH 730 Clovis, CA 731 Vancouver, WA 731 Loveland, CO 744 Bloomington, MN 755 New Braunfels, TX 758 Raleigh, NC 758 Evanston, IL 759 Warwick, RI 761 Hoover, AL 763 Virginia Beach, VA 770 Clifton, NJ 771 Edmond, OK 778 Gilbert, AZ 791 Round Rock, TX 793 Olathe, KS 799 Columbia, MD 828 Troy, MI 829 Overland Park, KS 831 Roswell, GA 838 Naperville, IL 841 Sugar Land, TX 854 Carmel, IN 872 Flower Mound, TX 875 Augusta, GA 633 Chattanooga, TN 634 Rapid City, SD 634 Cincinnati, OH 636 Roanoke, VA 636 St. Louis, MO 636 Orlando, FL 638 Anchorage, AK 639 Gainesville, FL 639 Santa Fe, NM 641 Greenville, NC 643 Little Rock, AR 644 Tulsa, OK 644 Athens, GA 646 Columbia, SC 646 Green Bay, WI 648 Knoxville, TN 648 Peoria, IL 651 Redding, CA 653 Wilmington, NC 653 Champaign, IL 655 Worcester, MA 656 Abilene, TX 659 Lynn, MA 661 Murfreesboro, TN 661 Davenport, IA 662 Wichita, KS 662 Billings, MT 663 Columbia, MO 663 Auburn, AL 666 Manchester, NH 669 Salem, OR 671 Tampa, FL 673 Greensboro, NC 674 Huntsville, AL 674 Tyler, TX 676 Winston-Salem, NC 676 Louisville, KY 678 Duluth, MN 679 Richmond, VA 680 Spokane, WA 683 Minneapolis, MN 685 Lynchburg, VA 687 Nampa, ID 687 Fayetteville, AR 689 Bloomington, IN 690 Danbury, CT 690 Tempe, AZ 692 Bethlehem, PA 696 Eugene, OR 696 Kennewick, WA 697 Colorado Springs, CO 698 Return to Main List Explore All Top 100 Best Places Cities Top 100 Cities Map

faqs.a: Mission Hospital is the largest hospital in the region and is nationally recognized for its quality of care. The hospital is also highly rated in several specialties and procedures, including cardiology, orthopedics and urology.

faqs.a: Dance to the beats of the Asheville Drum Circle, an informal gathering on Friday evenings in downtown Pritchard Park.

faqs.a: The Asheville Museum of Science captivates budding minds with its exhibits showcasing all the wonders of the natural world. Outdoor enthusiasts often rave over the North Carolina Arboretum, a hot spot where families can explore themed gardens and dive into all kinds of educational programs. The WNC Nature Center is another popular place with a reputation for teaching visitors about the area’s abundant native wildlife.

Then, end your afternoon by walking to The Chocolate Fetish for a sweet treat. The chocolate shop features award-winning, handmade chocolates and truffles.

The venue has hosted iconic artists like Bob Dylan and Joan Jett, as well as local and up-and-coming artists.

faqs.a: At Chai Pani, share nibbles and snacks reminiscent of those served by street vendors throughout India. Chai Pani even won a coveted James Beard “Outstanding Restaurant” award.
